title: Ligusticum mutellina
intro: Ligusticum mutellina, also known as meadow sweet or wild angelica, is a perennial plant in the carrot family that grows in mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits, as well as potential antimicrobial and antiviral effects. The bioactive constituents of Ligusticum mutellina include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Ligusticum mutellina is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to soothe digestive problems.
taxonomy: Ligusticum mutellina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. Its classification also includes it as a member of the genus Ligusticum, which comprises several other species of similar plants. The exact classification of Ligusticum mutellina is as follows: Ligusticum mutellina (formerly known as Seseli mutellinum), and it is further classified as a member of the order Apiales. This classification reflects the plant's botanical relationships and evolutionary history.
uses: Ligusticum mutellina, also known as Yarrow false chamomile, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. It is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also believed to have antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which may help to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. Additionally, Ligusticum mutellina is thought to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation. More research is needed to confirm the full range of medicinal uses for this plant.

constituents: Ligusticum mutellina, also known as Yarrow aikinii or Mutellina, contains various active constituents including volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The plant's volatile oils, which include ligustilide, are known for their medicinal properties. Flavonoids present in the plant include kaempferol and quercetin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. Additionally, phenolic acids such as ferulic acid and sinapic acid are also found in Ligusticum mutellina. These constituents contribute to the plant's therapeutic effects, although further research is needed to confirm their specific benefits.
parts: Ligusticum mutellina is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in relation to its roots. The roots of Ligusticum mutellina are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the roots are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The root extracts have also been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The roots are typically harvested in the fall, when the plant is in full maturity, and then dried for preservation and use in medicinal preparations.
